title: Messi and Ronaldo Are Building Tech Portfolios. Mo Salah Is Playing a Different Game
gist: Elite footballers are shifting from one-off endorsements to equity stakes as a post-career wealth strategy: Messi is assembling a Silicon Valley-style AI portfolio via Play Time HoldCo, Ronaldo concentrates on health tech (Whoop, Herbalife/Bioniq), while Salah sticks to traditional endorsements, property, and philanthropy.

insights.claim: Athletes are increasingly trading one-off endorsement fees for equity ownership stakes, driven by VC firms and startups that want celebrity investors offering global reach, credibility, and distribution rather than just capital.
insights.speaker: Omnia Al Desoukie
insights.so_what: Celebrity distribution is becoming a priced input in startup cap tables; watch for athlete-backed consumer brands.
insights.quote: equity—not sponsorship—is becoming one of sport's smartest plays.
insights.locator: Whether through venture capital, wearable technology

insights.claim: The endorsement-to-equity shift reflects a focus on long-term wealth creation and capital appreciation beyond an athlete's peak earning years, per Archers Valuation partner Kamraan Khan.
insights.speaker: Omnia Al Desoukie
insights.so_what: Frames athlete investing as a durable financial-planning trend, not a fad.
insights.quote: equity investments can provide the potential for capital appreciation and, where applicable, future dividend income, helping to build more sustainable wealth after retirement

insights.claim: Messi launched Play Time HoldCo in October 2022 with Viki founder Razmig Hovaghimian, initially targeting roughly $200 million to invest across sports, media, and technology.
insights.speaker: Omnia Al Desoukie
insights.so_what: A footballer running a $200M-target fund signals institutional-scale athlete capital.
insights.locator: In October 2022, Messi launched Play Time HoldCo

insights.claim: Play Time's portfolio has come to resemble a Silicon Valley venture fund, with AI bets including FieldAI, Fish Audio, World Labs, Perceptron, Intangible, and SuperAnnotate, plus sports plays Matchday and AC Momento.
insights.speaker: Omnia Al Desoukie
insights.so_what: Names specific private AI companies now carrying Messi's brand and cap-table credibility.
insights.quote: its bets include FieldAI, Fish Audio, World Labs, Perceptron, Intangible, and SuperAnnotate

insights.claim: Messi also holds equity in fantasy platform Sorare and joined the ownership group of KRÜ Esports, while his $20 million, three-year Socios.com role is a paid promotional contract rather than an equity stake.
insights.speaker: Omnia Al Desoukie
insights.so_what: Distinguishes genuine equity exposure from paid ambassadorships, useful for valuing athlete-brand deals.
insights.locator: Outside Play Time, Messi also holds an equity stake

insights.claim: Messi received an unprecedented ownership component in his 2023 Inter Miami move, and Sportico valued the club at $1.45 billion in February 2026, up 22 percent year-on-year and the highest valuation in MLS history.
insights.speaker: Omnia Al Desoukie
insights.so_what: Concrete valuation datapoint showing star-driven equity appreciation in sports franchises.
insights.quote: Sportico valued Inter Miami at $1.45 billion in February 2026, up 22 percent year-on-year and the highest valuation in MLS history.
insights.locator: As part of his landmark 2023 move

insights.claim: Ronaldo became a Whoop investor in May 2024 in what Whoop called one of his most significant investments to date, and Whoop's UAE expansion is backed by the Qatar Investment Authority and Mubadala Investment Company.
insights.speaker: Omnia Al Desoukie
insights.so_what: Ties Ronaldo and Gulf sovereign wealth to the wearables/health-analytics category.
insights.locator: Ronaldo became an investor in Whoop

insights.claim: Ronaldo paid $7.5 million for a 10 percent stake in HBL Pro2col Software (a Herbalife subsidiary) in February 2026, and Herbalife agreed a month later to acquire London-based Bioniq, an AI supplements firm Ronaldo already backed, in a deal worth up to $150 million.
insights.speaker: Omnia Al Desoukie
insights.so_what: Shows Ronaldo layering equity across a vertically integrating personalized-wellness stack with an M&A exit path.
insights.quote: Ronaldo's most concrete move came in February 2026, when he paid $7.5 million for a 10 percent stake in HBL Pro2col Software

insights.claim: Ronaldo reportedly received a 5 percent ownership stake in Saudi Pro League club Al-Nassr, finalized in June 2025 and valued at roughly £50 million ($66.7 million).
insights.speaker: Omnia Al Desoukie
insights.so_what: Another equity-for-star arrangement, aligning Ronaldo's wealth with Saudi sports investment.
insights.locator: Ronaldo has also reportedly received a 5 percent

insights.claim: Salah has largely avoided disclosed tech-startup investing, concentrating his interests in commercial holding companies, real estate, conventional endorsements (Adidas, Pepsi, Vodafone Egypt), and philanthropy.
insights.speaker: Omnia Al Desoukie
insights.so_what: A counterexample showing the equity shift is a choice, not a universal athlete default.
insights.locator: Salah, meanwhile, has taken a more traditional approach.
